posts.caption:
Why get a FITBIT or step tracker? Here's my main reasons why i'd recommend buying one and how to actually use them day to day. Don't spend loads of money for a super expensive one, as you'll likely not use most features after a couple weeks of getting it. PS... mine is the Fitbit versa 4 

#fitnesstracker #fitbit #steptrackers #fitnesstrackers

Do you have a WHY?
I’ve definitely noticed this clients that have the strongest why, stay the most committed and push themselves the hardest.

The biggest ones I’ve personally had are mostly kid related. People ideally want to set a good example for their kids, plus the fact having kids can drain most people 😅😵

#fitnesstips #fitdads #dadbod #selfimprovementjourney #selfimprovementdaily

posts.caption:
❌if you look like me on the left or in the swipes👉🏾, DON’T BULK!❌

I often get messages from overweight/chubby people, that are thinking to BULKING because they think that’s the only way to build muscle

I’d agree if your underweight then a slight surplus will be beneficial

BUT… if you are overweight:
You damn well do NOT need to be eating extra and increasing you bodyfat even more👀 (…if you’re overweight you CANNOT lean bulk) you are past that requirement.
Plus You will likely be able to build muscle AND lose fat by being in a slight calorie deficit as you weight train, especially if you are new to lifting(swipe to see an example)

If you’re just slightly chubby, then again don’t bulk but I’d say stay around your maintenance calories as you begin weight training and that’s enough

And of course in both instances lifting challenging weights overtime and ensure you are getting enough protein for your size and weight

To keep it ABSOLUTELY SIMPLE, for most men 5’7+ weighing over 160lbs+ aiming to get around 145g per day is a good minimum goal to have

Let me know your thoughts below👇🏾
Save and Share✅

#bulking #leanbulk #caloriecountinguk #caloriesurplus #bulkingseason #weightloss #buildingmuscles #buildmuscle #musclegain #overweightproblems #fatlossplan #gettingleaner #ukfitness #ukfitfamily #gymlife #fitnessjourney

posts.firstComment: I'm going from an overweight position and it took a long time to see results especially in areas of the most body fat. This can be very frustrating at the beginning but once you start seeing the results it's so satisfying as you know how hard you've had to work to get there.

latestComments.text: It's easier  starting out skinny, if the Goal is looking better,you usually get to see every pound of muscle immediately once it's created, and 1 to pounds of muscle and they a different person, most people I've seen who advocate for big bulks are twigs at first so the weight the gain typically looks great,

replies.text: @roksolidfitness yeah any form of muscle easily stands out

latestComments.text: @jjwp.fitness yes definitely hard going from skinny in terms of upping your calorie intake and building that strength and muscle. But seeing results quicker would be a great motivator. Going from overweight you have to reduce your calorie intake in a way you can stick to but also take longer to see the results which can be very demotivating for a lot of people causing them to quit. If you can maintain that drive though your sense of accomplishment in getting that weight off is so satisfying. There's definitely pros and cons to both sides.
@j.lynch81 yeah visual changes can take a fair bit of time

posts.caption:
👶🏽 The PAPI TJ era is now here😅💫

posts.firstComment: It's a drug for people with diabetes. There has been a shortage for people that seriously need it because people are too lazy to actually put in the time and effort to lose weight!

latestComments.text: It's a drug for people with diabetes. There has been a shortage for people that seriously need it because people are too lazy to actually put in the time and effort to lose weight!

Apparently it crushes all cravings including smoking and other addictions. Which can be a positive in the right direction, also could be good for those who do struggle with bad food habits and help those reprogram themselves to eat in a healthier way. Especially the morbidly obese n those who have autoimmune issues. But I agree if u just got a few pounds and are looking for a quick fix this isn’t the answer.
Not a fan of cheating and it is also causing shortages for those people who this medication was actually designed for.

posts.caption:
⚠️ Create Non-Negotiables! Whether its daily or weekly, set some simple and straight forward goals that align with your fitness journey and hold yourself accountable for doing them. These can help you from veering off path. Mines pretty simple but has helped me a lot over the years. Outside of recently (👶🏽) i've basically stuck to this, even when travelling 💪 

#fitnesstips #selfimprovement
posts.firstComment: Non negotiables for the win 🙌

latestComments.text: Non negotiables for the win 🙌

posts.caption:
How to get BACK in the gym and get your gains back?
Simple. Do the things that got you them in the first place but the extra benefit is, its quicker to get your muscle back once you've already had them before.
- Set realistic goals, stay consistent
- Rest and recovery are just as important! 
- Track your progress, even the small wins, to keep that motivation up.
- Remember, it’s all about showing up and getting back into the flow, then build back your fitness level one step at a time💪
#gymtips

💭Do you agree with his take?
